description: Fix two CLI UX issues found during fresh-user testing: (1) config set port notifies the wrong port causing a false 'Server not running' message, and (2) doctor gives wrong advice when another service occupies the default port.

userStories.title: Fix config set port notification targeting new port instead of running server's port
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set port <new>` to correctly notify the running server on its current port so that I see an accurate message instead of a false 'Server not running'.
acceptanceCriteria: When the server is running on port 9515 and the user runs `opentabs config set port 8080`, the CLI sends the reload notification to port 9515 (the old/running port), not port 8080
The confirmation message says something like 'Server running on port 9515. Restart to apply port change.' instead of 'Server not running — changes will apply on next start.'
When the server is genuinely not running, the existing 'Server not running' message still appears
When no port change occurs (e.g. setting the same port), behavior is unchanged
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, function handleSetPort (around line 455-474). The bug is on line 473: `await notifyServer({ port: options.port, warnIfNotRunning: true })`. After writing the new port to config (line 470), `notifyServer` resolves the port by reading from the newly-written config file (since options.port is undefined). Fix: before writing the new config, save the old port value (from `config.port ?? DEFAULT_PORT`), then after writing pass the old port explicitly to notifyServer: `await notifyServer({ port: oldPort, warnIfNotRunning: true })`. Also update the message: when the server IS reachable on the old port but the port was changed, print a restart-needed message mentioning the old port. The notifyServer utility is in platform/cli/src/lib/notify-server.ts — check how it returns success/failure to know if the server was actually reached.

userStories.title: Fix doctor advice when another service occupies the configured port
userStories.description: As a user, I want the doctor command to give correct advice when another service is on the port, so that I don't follow a suggestion that will immediately fail.
acceptanceCriteria: When doctor detects a non-OpenTabs service on the port, the hint says something like 'Stop the other service, or use a different port: opentabs start --port <number>' instead of 'Start it with: opentabs start'
When the port is completely unreachable (nothing running), the existing 'not reachable' message and 'Start it with: opentabs start' hint remain unchanged
The hint uses the configured port in the --port suggestion only when it differs from the default
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts, function checkServerHealth (around line 77-110). The problematic lines are ~95-97 where the hint says `Start it with: opentabs start${portSuffix}` for the 'another service is running' case. This advice will fail because the port is already occupied. Change the hint to suggest stopping the other service or using a different port. Note there are TWO hint assignments in this function: one for 'another service' (line ~95) and one for 'not reachable' (line ~105 in the catch block). Only the 'another service' hint needs to change — the 'not reachable' hint is correct (nothing is on the port, so starting on it will succeed).
